**Marugbo**, also called **eweta** or **obnettette**,[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) is leafy vegetable (*Clerodendrum volubile*) from western Africa.[[3]](#cite_note-:2-3)

## Characteristics

[edit | edit source]

## Selection and storage

[edit | edit source]

The leaves are typically available dried.[[3]](#cite_note-:2-3) They are therefore best stored in an airtight container away from moisture.

## Use

[edit | edit source]

The leaves are eaten as a vegetable, usually cooked in various soups.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)[[3]](#cite_note-:2-3)

[edit | edit source]

Category *Recipes using marugbo* not found
